name: Circuit Breaker
def: A proxy that stops calling a failing dependency for a cool-down period to prevent cascading failure.
problem: Stop hammering an unhealthy downstream service and let it recover, while failing fast for callers.
mechanism: Tracks failures; on a threshold it "opens" (fails fast without calling), after a timeout goes "half-open" to test, and "closes" again on success.
benefits: Fails fast instead of piling up timeouts
limits: Can mask real issues if too aggressive
failures: Flapping between states under marginal load
Tripping on transient blips and harming availability
myths: "Circuit breakers prevent failures" — they contain blast radius, not root causes
